narrative.headline: 2022 Jeep Cherokee: power train tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 42 complaints with NHTSA. The power train is by far the most reported area at 50%, followed by the electrical system and engine (17% each). No crashes, fires, or deaths are reported across any area, and the only injury (1) shows up under steering. Several power train reports describe sudden loss of power while driving, so it is worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 21 reports (50%), half of all complaints. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported, but owners describe sudden loss of power while driving. Test acceleration and watch for warning lights.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 7 reports (17%), tied with the engine for second most reported. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Owners mention unexpected behavior like shifting into manual mode on its own.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 7 reports (17%), tied for second most reported. No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Watch for loss of power or warning lights while driving.

issues.title: Exterior Lighting
issues.blurb: 4 reports (10%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners report failed turn indicators. Check that all exterior lights work.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: The fewest reports at 3 (7%), but the only area with an injury (1). No crashes or fires. Owners mention front axle problems, so check steering feel on a test drive.
